black and white: Clear-cut; involving only two opposing sides with no gray area.
kick sb out: To force someone to leave a place.
watch out for sth/sb: Be careful of something or somebody.
better off: Be in a more favorable situation or position.
take sth out of context: Misrepresent the meaning of something by amating the circumstances of it.
cut sb off: Interrupt and stop somebody from talking.
check sth out: Examine something or visit a place to learn about it.
hold sth/sb up: Delay or stop something or somebody.
catch up: Meet up to update each other.
attention-seeking: Deliberately behaving in a way to attract attention.
rusty: Not as good as somebody or something was.
downer: Something or somebody that makes you unhappy.
